methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: HistoSonics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.25B

private.name: Formation Bio
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $1.7B+

data.narrative:
Synchron is a Series D biotech company founded by Tom Oxley and Rahul Sharma that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 8 private peers in Biotech & Health at similar or adjacent stages, including HistoSonics, Formation Bio, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics, alongside 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $200M raised as a value proxy, implying $300M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector peers at Series C through Series E yields a median valuation of $2.3B, with a range of $1.5B to $5.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies. The peer set reflects meaningful dispersion: Formation Bio (Series D) valued at $1.7B sits at the lower end, while Abridge (Series E) reaches $5.3B.

Triangulating these two methods produces a low estimate of $255M, a median of $1.3B, and a high of $2.7B. The wide band reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation multiples and the heterogeneity of biotech exit prospects. Key uncertainties include clinical validation timing, intellectual property defensibility, and path to near-term revenue. The peer-median approach—anchored to directly comparable stage and sector—carries greater weight than stage-progression alone, suggesting the $1.3B–$2.3B range warrants primary focus.
